# Find Me People

> Free browser extension that surfaces hidden customer service emails and phone numbers on any website -- no account, no data collection, 100% local processing.

## What it does

Find Me People auto-scans every page you visit, extracts contact info buried behind chatbot walls, ranks results by likelihood of reaching a real human support agent, and lets you compose an email or place a call without leaving the page. A side panel pull-tab on every page surfaces the same ranked emails and phones, business hours, copy history, and support pages. Templates for refunds, complaints, cancellations, billing, and support are built in. Phone numbers deep-link into your dialer of choice -- system phone, WhatsApp, Google Voice, FaceTime, or Microsoft Teams. Reads structured data and hydration state (__NEXT_DATA__, JSON-LD, schema.org) for contacts hidden in JS, scans same-origin iframes, and falls back to fetching common contact-page URLs (/contact, /support, /about) when the visible page is sparse.

## Key facts

- Version: 1.5.2
- License: MIT (open source)
- Privacy: no accounts, no telemetry, no third-party tracking. Network requests limited to same-origin fetches of common contact-page URLs (/contact, /support, /about, /help) when the in-page scan finds zero contacts. credentials: 'omit', once per origin per session.
- Permissions: activeTab, scripting, storage. Host permissions: <all_urls> (required because contact info can appear on any site).
- Browsers: Chrome, Edge, Brave, Arc, Opera, and Firefox 121+
- Developer: Matt Duke (matthewduke0@gmail.com)

## Links

- Homepage: https://find-me-people.com
- Privacy policy: https://find-me-people.com/privacy.html
- Chrome Web Store: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/find-me-people/ngfklhkcicocfchdmepiajdmboialikf
- Firefox Add-ons: https://addons.mozilla.org/addon/find-me-people/
- Source code: https://github.com/MatthewDuke1/find-me-people

## Why it exists

Customer service is being rationed. Companies bury contact info behind chatbot walls, knowing most people give up before reaching a person. The average American spends 10-15 minutes searching for a real support contact and loses 45+ hours per year to support search and waiting. Find Me People exists to break that bet -- one click instead of a scavenger hunt.
